P.description: The smallest quantity of topsoil bags that should be employed

B.description: The quantity of water needed to moisturize one bag of topsoil daily.

D.description: The highest quantity of bags containing a mixture of topsoil and subsoil

Z.description: Quantity of water needed to keep one bag of subsoil hydrated on a daily basis

K.description: The highest percentage of bags that can contain topsoil.

assumptions.description: The smallest quantity of topsoil bags that should be employed must be non-negative.
The quantity of water needed to moisturize one bag of topsoil daily must be non-negative.
The highest quantity of bags containing a mixture of topsoil and subsoil must be non-negative.
The quantity of water needed to keep one bag of subsoil hydrated on a daily basis must be non-negative.
The highest percentage of bags that can contain topsoil must be non-negative.

d.description: The quantity of bags containing topsoil

constraints.description: The percentage of topsoil bags should not go beyond K out of all bags.
The number of subsoil bags must be non-negative.
The number of topsoil bags must be non-negative.

objective.description: The total amount of water needed consists of the combined value of (Z multiplied by the quantity of subsoil bags) and (B multiplied by the quantity of topsoil bags). The aim is to reduce the overall water consumption as much as possible.

notes: Arbitrarily remove constraints from the original formulation.
